danger_signals.meaning: Progression from safe to hazardous: 'Motocross Circuit' (beginner) → 'Radical Circuit' → 'Nightmare Circuit' → 'Doom Circuit' → 'Forbidden Circuit' (expert/dangerous)
Higher displacement = greater speed, less control, higher crash risk

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Named rider characters (Nails, Hawk, Fang) serve as mechanical identity variants rather than faction markers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rider selection establishes cosmetic/performance identity in motocross sport context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Motocross Circuit environment + 50cc engine class UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Slower bike acceleration, forgiving physics, wider track margins
Increased speed, tighter handling requirements, technical track design

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nightmare/Doom/Forbidden Circuit naming conventions + 250cc Pro class
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Maximum speed, punishing collision physics, extreme track obstacles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI labels append '(Co-Op)' to all circuit categories
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: World-state shifts from solo competition to shared environment

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression from mundane ('Motocross') → extreme ('Nightmare', 'Doom', 'Forbidden') communicates escalating danger and challenge without explicit warnings
'Kiddies' designation establishes safe learning track; Pro class signals expertise requirement
Named riders (Nails, Hawk, Fang) establish character identity; selection implies playstyle variant

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: High-speed, sensitive steering, punishing collision response

notes: Dirt Trax FX communicates world-state primarily through UI taxonomy and naming conventions rather than dynamic visual environmental changes. The game uses a clear progression ladder system where danger escalates through circuit naming (mundane → nightmare) and engine displacement (50cc → 250cc). No faction warfare or political systems detected; world-state is purely difficulty-based. The speedrun.com leaderboard data provides category structure but minimal visual documentation of actual in-game manifestations.
